text: A practical and atom-economical protocol for the synthesis of highly valuable benzoxepine skeletons has been developed via the formal (5 + 2) cycloaddition of readily available o-vinylphenols and alkynes. Catalyzed by a combination of [Cp*RhCl2]2 and Cu(OAc)2, the transformation proceeds through the selective cleavage of the terminal C–H bond of the alkenyl moiety. This versatile platform can be further extended by substituting the alkyne with carbon monoxide, facilitating a formal (5 + 1) cycloaddition that yields substituted coumarin derivatives.
